records.ety: [OE. envenimen, F. envenimer; pref. en- (L. in) + F. venin poison. See Venom.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Envenomed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Envenoming.]

defs: To taint or impregnate with venom, or any substance noxious to life; to poison; to render dangerous or deadly by poison, as food, drink, a weapon; as, envenomed meat, wine, or arrow; also, to poison (a person) by impregnating with venom.
quotes: O, what a world is this, when what is comely Envenoms him that bears it!

defs: To taint or impregnate with bitterness, malice, or hatred; to imbue as with venom; to imbitter.
quotes: On the question of slavery opinion has of late years been peculiarly envenomed.
